response: In simple terms, gravity is the invisible force that pulls every object with mass toward one another. It is why apples fall from trees, why we stay planted on the ground, and why the Moon keeps circling the Earth. The more massive an object is, and the closer you are to it, the stronger its pull. This same force governs the orbits of planets around the Sun, shapes the paths of galaxies, and even causes ocean tides by drawing on Earth's waters. You can picture it as an unseen tether linking all matter across the universe, constantly drawing things together. That is gravity.
